question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the System.currentTimeMillis() clock used in statementInspector being subject to NTP adjustments or clock skew, causing the elapsed time calculation to be incorrect and either never triggering the timeout (allowing unbounded queries) or always triggering it (causing a DoS partition)?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that causes the EVM to execute a loop of CALL opcodes, each triggering a new SQL lookup, where the cumulative time across all lookups exceeds requestTimeout but each individual lookup is fast, causing the timeout to fire mid-loop and leave the EVM execution in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a payload of exactly maxPayloadLogSize * 10 bytes (3000 bytes) to fill the ContentCachingRequestWrapper buffer, and then send thousands of such requests concurrently to exhaust the servlet container's memory and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the QueryTimeoutException thrown by statementInspector is a Spring DataAccessException, which may be retried by certain Spring retry configurations, causing a single timed-out request to generate multiple DB queries and amplify the partition effect?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call with a deeply nested contract invocation chain that causes the EVM to issue SQL queries in a recursive pattern, where the total elapsed time across all recursive calls exceeds requestTimeout, causing the timeout to fire at an arbitrary depth and leave the call stack in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContent()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request payload that is a valid but extremely long hex string (just under maxPayloadLogSize * 10 = 3000 chars), causing the ContentCachingRequestWrapper to buffer the full payload and the LoggingFilter to attempt GZIP compression on it, amplifying memory and CPU usage per request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the statementInspector lambda captures 'timeout' as a long at bean creation time (web3Properties.getRequestTimeout().toMillis()), meaning any runtime change to requestTimeout is not reflected, and craft requests that exploit the fixed timeout value to cause a sustained network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that triggers a SQL query with a very large IN clause (e.g., thousands of contract addresses), causing the query to take longer than requestTimeout and the statementInspector to throw QueryTimeoutException, but only after the DB has already allocated significant resources for the query?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doFilterInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes the filterChain.doFilter() to block for the full requestTimeout duration (10s) before the statementInspector fires, causing the servlet thread to be held for 10 seconds per request and exhausting the thread pool with only (thread_pool_size / 10) requests/sec?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the statementInspector only checks elapsed time and does not enforce a per-statement timeout, allowing a single SQL statement to run indefinitely if it was started before the timeout threshold was reached, causing an unbounded DB partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user infer the requestTimeout value (10s default) from the 503 Service Unavailable response timing, and use this information to craft requests that are designed to complete in exactly 9.9 seconds, maximizing DB connection hold time while avoiding the timeout, causing a sustained network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that causes the EVM to execute a STATICCALL to a precompile that triggers a DB lookup, where the DB lookup is slow due to a network partition between the web3 service and the DB, causing the statementInspector to fire and return a 503, masking the underlying partition from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests that all hit the requestTimeout boundary simultaneously, causing a thundering herd of QueryTimeoutExceptions that overwhelm the error handling path and cause the web3 service to become unresponsive, effectively partitioning it from all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContent()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a payload that contains a crafted string that defeats the DATA_PATTERN regex in reorderFields(), causing the regex to backtrack excessively (ReDoS) and hold the logging thread for an extended period, contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the statementInspector uses System.currentTimeMillis() (wall clock) rather than a monotonic clock, and by sending requests during a period of NTP clock adjustment (clock jumping forward), cause the elapsed time to appear larger than requestTimeout immediately, causing all requests to fail with QueryTimeoutException and partitioning all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that triggers a SQL query involving a JOIN across multiple large tables, where the query planner chooses a hash join that requires significant memory, causing the DB to spill to disk and the query to exceed requestTimeout, partitioning the DB connection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doFilterInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes an IOException during filterChain.doFilter(), which is caught by the catch(Exception t) block in LoggingFilter, but the response is already partially written, causing the client to receive a truncated response that appears as a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Positive constraint on maxPayloadLogSize (minimum value 1) to understand that if the configuration is set to 1, the ContentCachingRequestWrapper buffer would be only 10 bytes, causing all requests with larger payloads to be truncated and potentially causing incorrect behavior that appears as a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that triggers a SQL query that acquires a row-level lock, and then the statementInspector fires and throws QueryTimeoutException before the transaction is committed, leaving the lock held until the transaction is rolled back, partitioning other users from that row?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that causes the EVM to execute a CREATE2 opcode that triggers a DB lookup for the new contract address, where the lookup is slow due to index contention, causing the statementInspector to fire and abort the transaction, leaving the contract creation in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the statementInspector is a Hibernate-level interceptor and does not enforce timeouts at the JDBC driver level, meaning that a slow DB response (e.g., due to a network partition between web3 and DB) will not be interrupted until the next SQL statement boundary, allowing the connection to hang indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContent()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a payload that is exactly at the maxPayloadLogSize boundary (300 chars) but contains characters that expand when processed by StringUtils.deleteWhitespace(), causing the content to exceed the boundary and trigger the compression path unexpectedly?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that causes the EVM to execute a DELEGATECALL chain that triggers multiple DB lookups in sequence, where each lookup is just under the timeout threshold but the total time exceeds requestTimeout, causing the timeout to fire mid-chain and leave the EVM state inconsistent?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statementInspector()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that triggers a SQL query that causes a deadlock in the database, where the deadlock resolution takes longer than requestTimeout, causing the statementInspector to fire and throw QueryTimeoutException before the deadlock is resolved, leaving the transaction in an inconsistent state?
